Deep ontological exploration explaining why States must possess causal history and algorithmic inertia (quán tính logic). Clarifies the "laziness" of reality as an optimization principle under Logic: minimizing interaction cost, preserving coherence, and enabling stable evolution. Connects this to universe formation from chaos and the necessity of both unity and differentiation. Serves as a critical extension to the Fundamental States Ontology. Public domain.
